senses.definition: having no space left

examples.x: The restaurant was full, so we had to wait for a table to become available.
After a day of shopping, her shopping bags were full of new purchases.
The parking lot was full, forcing us to find parking on a nearby street.
The storage container was full to the brim with old clothes and belongings.
The bus was full, so we had to stand in the aisle during the journey.

senses.definition: with no one or nothing inside

examples.x: The empty room echoed with every step, its walls bare and devoid of furniture.
The empty bottle rolled across the floor, its contents completely consumed.
She opened the empty refrigerator, realizing she needed to go grocery shopping.
The empty suitcase sat in the corner, waiting to be filled with clothes for the trip.
The empty gas tank left them stranded on the side of the road, miles from the nearest gas station.

senses.definition: needed to be done for a particular reason or purpose

examples.x: It is necessary to study for exams to achieve good grades.
Wearing sunscreen is necessary to protect your skin from harmful UV rays.
Proper hydration is necessary for maintaining good health.
Following safety protocols is necessary to prevent accidents in the workplace.
Clear communication is necessary for effective collaboration in a team.
Having the right tools is necessary to complete the project efficiently.

senses.definition: not needed at all or more than what is required

examples.x: His comments during the meeting were unnecessary and only served to prolong the discussion.
Adding extra sugar to the recipe would be unnecessary since it's already sweet enough.
The additional security measures seemed unnecessary given the low risk of a security breach.
Buying a brand new car for a short commute is unnecessary; a used one would suffice.
Using overly complicated language in the presentation was unnecessary; the audience would have understood simpler terms.

senses.definition: not involving difficulty in doing or understanding

examples.x: The recipe was simple, requiring only a few ingredients and basic cooking techniques.
The concept was simple to grasp; it required only a basic understanding of the topic.
The task was simple to complete; it didn't require any specialized skills.
Solving the puzzle was simple; it only required logical thinking and observation.
The instructions were simple to follow, with clear steps outlined.

senses.definition: needing a lot of skill or effort to do

examples.x: Learning to play the piano at a professional level is hard and requires years of practice.
Writing a novel can be hard, as it requires creativity, discipline, and perseverance.
Mastering a new language can be hard, especially if it has a complex grammar and vocabulary.
Performing surgery is hard and requires precise skills and years of medical training.
Completing a marathon is hard, but many people train hard to achieve this goal.

senses.definition: indicating the greatest or highest possible amount, quantity, or degree

examples.x: The maximum capacity of the venue is 500 people.
He reached the maximum speed of the car on the highway.
The company sets a maximum limit on the number of vacation days employees can take each year.
She received the maximum score on the exam, demonstrating her mastery of the subject.
The website allows users to upload files up to a maximum size of 10 megabytes.

senses.definition: having the least or smallest amount possible

examples.x: The minimum requirement for admission to the program is a bachelor's degree.
He received the minimum score needed to pass the exam.
The minimum age for purchasing alcohol is 21 in many countries.

senses.definition: not far from a place

examples.x: The near bus stop is convenient for commuting to work.
The near side of the riverbank offers a beautiful view of the sunset.
She moved to the near city to be closer to her workplace.

senses.definition: situated at a considerable distance in space

examples.x: The village lay on the far side of the river, barely visible through the mist.
They trekked to a far island untouched by modern development.
The spacecraft traveled to a far corner of the solar system.
A lighthouse marked the far end of the rocky coastline.
From the hilltop, they admired the far peaks outlined against the sky.

senses.definition: regular and without any exceptional features

examples.x: The meeting was filled with common tasks and discussions.
Her style was simple and common, perfect for everyday wear.
The restaurant offered common dishes like pasta and salad.
It was a common day at the office with no surprises.
His response was so common that it didn’t stand out in the conversation.

senses.definition: not happening or found often

examples.x: Seeing a shooting star in the city is uncommon due to light pollution.
Finding parking downtown is uncommon during the evening rush hour.
Severe weather like hurricanes is uncommon in the northern part of the country.
Snowfall is uncommon in this region.
His uncommon talent for solving complex problems impressed everyone.
It's not uncommon for people to feel nervous before a big presentation.

senses.definition: having so many things to do in a way that leaves not much free time

examples.x: As a student, Jenny is always busy with assignments, exams, and extracurricular activities.
The project deadline is approaching, and the team is getting busy with finalizing reports and presentations.
In the bustling city, people are constantly busy with work, errands, and social commitments.
As a small business owner, Mark is always busy with managing orders, inventory, and customer inquiries.
The event planner became exceptionally busy with coordinating logistics and ensuring everything ran smoothly.

senses.definition: having no particular plans or tasks

examples.x: She enjoyed a free afternoon, allowing her to relax and read a book in the park.
He felt a sense of liberation as he walked out of work, knowing he had the evening free to spend with his family.
The weekend was finally here, and they had two whole days free to explore the city.
The empty calendar on her phone signaled a rare opportunity to have a free day with no appointments or obligations.

senses.definition: avoiding work or activity and preferring to do as little as possible

examples.x: Her room was always messy because she was too lazy to tidy up after herself.
The employee's lazy work ethic resulted in missed deadlines and subpar performance evaluations.
Rather than cooking a meal, he opted for takeout because he was feeling too lazy to cook.
He missed out on opportunities for advancement at work because he was too lazy to pursue additional training or certifications.
The lazy student consistently skipped classes and failed to complete assignments on time.

senses.definition: (of a person) putting in a lot of effort and dedication to achieve goals or complete tasks
helping our body or mind rest

examples.x: Reading a book in a cozy armchair can be a relaxing way to unwind before bed.
The warm, bubbling water in the hot tub was incredibly relaxing, easing tense muscles.

senses.definition: causing mental or emotional strain or worry due to pressure or demands

examples.x: The workload at her new job was incredibly stressful.
Planning a wedding can be a stressful experience.
Moving to a new city can be stressful, especially when you don't know anyone.
Studying for exams is always stressful for students.
Waiting for the test results was a stressful time for the patient and their family.
The job interview was a stressful experience for him.

senses.definition: struggling to learn or understand things quickly

examples.x: Her dumb decision to skip class resulted in failing grades and academic probation.
Despite repeated explanations, she remained dumb to the complexities of the subject.
The dumb student found it challenging to keep up with the pace of the class.
His dumb decisions often led to unfavorable outcomes, as he failed to consider the consequences.
The dumb criminal left behind ample evidence, making it easy for the police to apprehend him.

senses.definition: (of clothes or furniture) making us feel physically relaxed

examples.x: After a long day, I like to change into comfortable pajamas.
The sofa in the living room is so comfortable; I could relax on it for hours.
She prefers wearing comfortable sneakers when walking long distances.
The bed was made with soft, comfortable sheets that ensured a good night's sleep.
The oversized armchair in the corner of the room looked invitingly comfortable.
He opted for a comfortable hoodie and sweatpants for the lazy Sunday afternoon.

senses.definition: (of clothes, furniture, etc.) unpleasant to use or wear

examples.x: The tight jeans were uncomfortable to wear for long periods.
The old chair was so uncomfortable that no one wanted to sit in it.
The scratchy fabric of the sweater made it uncomfortable against her skin.
The dining chairs were uncomfortable, causing guests to fidget during the meal.
She found the high heels uncomfortable to walk in, so she switched to flats.

senses.definition: going from one place to another in a straight line without stopping or changing direction

examples.x: The direct route to the airport takes approximately twenty minutes by car.
She prefers to take the direct path through the forest rather than the winding trails.
The river flows in a direct course from the mountains to the sea.
The train offers a direct route from the city to the countryside.

senses.definition: not going in a straight line or the shortest way

examples.x: Taking the scenic route was enjoyable but more indirect than the highway.
The indirect route through the mountains offered breathtaking views but was challenging to navigate.
The GPS recommended an indirect route to avoid heavy traffic on the main highway.
The road construction forced us to take an indirect route to our destination.
The indirect path through the forest was longer but offered a more peaceful and serene experience.

senses.definition: different or better than what is normal

examples.x: That song holds a special place in her heart.
The team worked hard to create a special experience for their guests.
Their special relationship blossomed into a deep and meaningful friendship over the years.
They decorated the room with balloons to make it look special.
The special occasion called for a celebration with family and friends.

senses.definition: not unusual or different in any way

examples.x: His weekend routine was ordinary, consisting of household chores and relaxing at home.
The meal she cooked was ordinary, with no special ingredients or flavors.
The neighborhood park was ordinary, with standard playground equipment and benches.
His taste in clothing was ordinary, preferring simple and casual attire.
The movie plot was ordinary, following a predictable storyline with no surprises.

senses.definition: known by a lot of people

examples.x: The famous singer performed to a sold-out crowd at the arena.
The famous author's novels have been translated into multiple languages.
Tourists flock to the city to visit famous landmarks such as the Eiffel Tower.
The famous actor won numerous awards for his performances in blockbuster movies.
She became famous overnight after her viral video gained millions of views.

senses.definition: (of a person) not known by many people

examples.x: The band played in small venues for years, struggling to gain recognition and remain relatively unknown in the music industry.
Despite her talent, the singer remained relatively unknown until her song went viral on social media.
The unknown artist's work was displayed in a small, local gallery.
The concert featured a few unknown bands, which surprised the audience.

senses.definition: having a great distance from the surface to the bottom

examples.x: The ocean is incredibly deep, with some parts reaching depths of over 36,000 feet.
The lake was deep, reaching depths that were difficult to measure.
The hole they dug in the garden was deep enough to plant the tree securely.
The canyon was deep, with towering walls that seemed to stretch endlessly.
Can you tell me how deep this well is before we lower the bucket?
The pool is eight feet deep at the diving end.
How deep is the lake at its deepest point?
They drilled a hole that was two meters deep to reach the underground pipes.

senses.definition: having a short distance from the surface to the bottom

examples.x: She planted the flower seeds in a shallow hole in the garden.
The dish should be baked in a shallow pan to ensure even cooking.
The pond is so shallow that you can easily touch the bottom with your feet.
The river became shallow during the dry season, exposing rocks and sandbars.
